explanation: This command disables Windows Defender behavior and real-time monitoring using PowerShell. It matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria by using PowerShell and the '-DisableBehaviorMonitoring' and '-DisableRealTimeMonitoring' flags.
This command uses the shorter form of the PowerShell CLI (pwsh) and equivalent syntax to disable monitoring. It still matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command configures the Windows Defender service to be disabled using sc.exe. It matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria by using sc.exe and the 'config' command with 'start=disabled'.
This command stops the Windows Defender service using sc.exe. It matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria by using sc.exe and the 'stop' command.
This command deletes the Windows Defender service using sc.exe. It matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria by using sc.exe and the 'delete' command.
